description: OWASP Top 10:2025 A03 module: Composer/PHP supply-chain integrity checks. Targets the Laravel-Lang attack class — autoload.files injection, mutable git-tag pinning, and PHP dropper patterns.

rules.title: autoload.files injection vector (Composer)
rules.description: composer.json declares autoload.files entries. PHP executes every listed file automatically via the Composer autoloader on each application boot. This was the precise injection mechanism in the May 2026 Laravel-Lang supply chain attack: attackers added src/helpers.php to autoload.files inside rewritten git tags, achieving immediate code execution without any explicit call site.

rules.title: Missing composer.lock (Composer)
rules.description: composer.json found but no composer.lock committed. A missing lock file means every install re-resolves dependencies from registries, making mutable-tag attacks like Laravel-Lang silently effective. The lock file — committed and audited — is the primary integrity control for Composer projects.

rules.title: Tag-only pin in composer.lock (Composer)
rules.description: One or more packages in composer.lock are pinned by a mutable version tag rather than a commit SHA. The Laravel-Lang attacker exploited tag mutability: by force-pushing malicious code behind existing version tags, they bypassed version-pinned installs entirely. Packages must be locked to an immutable commit SHA.

rules.title: PHP base64/obfuscated eval dropper (Composer)
rules.description: An autoloaded PHP file contains eval(base64_decode(...)) or equivalent obfuscation. This is the canonical PHP dropper signature and matches the payload style used in the Laravel-Lang credential stealer.

rules.title: Remote fetch in autoloaded PHP file (Composer)
rules.description: An autoloaded PHP file fetches content from a remote URL. Combined with auto-execution via autoload.files, this recreates the dropper→stager→stealer chain observed in the Laravel-Lang attack.
